name: pdf-preview-next
description: Modern successor to vscode-pdf: fast, secure PDF viewing in VS Code with PDF.js 5, live reload, search, outline, dark modes, and reliable preview state.

commands.title: PDF Preview Next: Reset View State

pdf-preview.default.cursor.markdownDescription: The default cursor tool for preview.

pdf-preview.default.scale.markdownDescription: The default zoom level for preview. Allowed values are `auto`, `page-actual`, `page-fit`, `page-width`, and 1-based scale values. (`1.0` means 100%)

pdf-preview.default.sidebar.markdownDescription: Open the PDF sidebar by default when the active sidebar panel is available.

pdf-preview.default.sidebarPanel.markdownDescription: The sidebar panel to show when the PDF sidebar opens. `pdf-preview.default.sidebar` still controls whether the sidebar opens by default.

pdf-preview.default.scrollMode.markdownDescription: The default scroll mode for preview.

pdf-preview.default.spreadMode.markdownDescription: The default spread mode for preview.

pdf-preview.reload.closeOnDelete.markdownDescription: Close the preview when the opened PDF is deleted. Disable this for TeX-style build workflows that temporarily delete and recreate the PDF.

pdf-preview.reload.debounceMs.markdownDescription: Milliseconds to wait after PDF file change notifications before refreshing the preview. Increase this if your build tool writes PDFs in multiple bursts.

pdf-preview.appearance.theme.markdownDescription: Preview appearance. `auto` follows VS Code colors for viewer chrome, `dark` uses dark viewer chrome while leaving PDF pages unchanged, `night` asks PDF.js to recolor rendered PDF pages for lower-eye-strain reading, `reader` is reserved for a smarter color-preserving reader mode and currently uses the safe night rendering path, `dark-pages` is a compatibility alias for `night`, and `inverted` applies full-page CSS inversion as a fallback for scanned or image-heavy PDFs.

pdf-preview.printCommand.markdownDescription: Custom command line for printing PDFs. Use `{{file}}` as a placeholder for the PDF path; when omitted, the path is appended as the last argument. The command is executed without a shell. When empty, the extension tries `lp` then falls back to the system viewer.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run bundle && npm run test:compat
